task_completion.description: Open Nx Witness and view the Walkway Camera
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Nx Witness client interface is visible, displaying a video feed labeled 'Walkway Camera'.
The video feed shows a pedestrian walkway with an unauthorized object such as a bicycle, cart, skateboard, or other vehicle.
The Nx Witness timeline or bookmark panel displays one or more bookmarks explicitly named 'Zone Violation'.

task_completion.description: Create or open the violation report file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal is open showing the file path '/home/ga/violation_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: Document timestamps and object types in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows content containing timestamps paired with object types (e.g., 'bicycle', 'cart', 'skateboard').

integrity.description: The agent used the Nx Witness GUI to view the video and create bookmarks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Nx Witness application interface being interacted with, rather than terminal commands or API scripts being used to create bookmarks.

integrity.description: The agent manually created the report based on video observations
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using a text editor to write the report, rather than running a script to auto-generate the file.

integrity.description: The agent actually reviewed the video footage to find anomalies
integrity.visual_evidence: The timeline in Nx Witness shows the agent scrubbing through or playing the video to locate the violations.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The camera name is 'Walkway Camera'. The video file is 'ucsd_pedestrian.mp4' from the UCSDped2 dataset. The bookmarks must be named exactly 'Zone Violation'. The report must be saved to '/home/ga/violation_report.txt'.
